text:
Person1: In so many words, yes.
Person2: Then I don't think so Mr. Bloom. If nothing is going to change, I'd just as soon it not change in the way it hasn't been changing all this time.
Person1: It's not like you're going to lose anything. You can ask anyone in town. I've been nothing if not generous. I want the best for everyone.

Person1: I -- hate -- you.
Person2: Well I guess that makes us even.
Person1: Ha!  Don't make me laugh.  You're crazy about me!  That's why you lied and cheated to get near me!
Person2: Oh come on, you're the one who came after me -- the swarthy, earthy, sensual worker!
Person1: I was slumming it!  I danced with you because you were the most pathetic specimen in the place!
Person2: Is that the same standard you used to choose General Formica?
Person1: I didn't choose him.  What kind  of idiot would...  ...choose who she wanted to marry?

Person1: You let me make a fool of myself! You lied to me! I dont know why that surprises me because lying is what you do. Its your job. Its who you are. How would you even know if something were real?
Person2: Youre judging me?! Oh, thats a good one. Youre lying to me, youre lying to your family, and youre lying to yourself. But youre going to stand there and point a finger at me? The man you hired to pretend to be your boyfriend?

Person1: Hello?
Person2: Hey. What are you doing? How are you?
Person1: I'm fine. Who is this?
Person2: Georgia.
Person1: Hi....what....what's up....?
Person2: It's ok that I'm calling, right, I mean? It's ok.
Person1: Yeah. No. It's ok. What's goin' on?
Person2: I just wanted to call and talk to you, thank you for last night, try and get you before you went to work and say, "hey."
Person1: I'm going to work.
Person2: Uhhh...I am sooo tired...I stayed up too late last night, what about you, when did you go to sleep?
Person1: Not very late.
Person2: You're going to work now?
Person1: Yes.
Person2: Can I ask you a question?
Person1: Uh-huh.
Person2: Remember last night I was talking to you and I was telling you about my apartment, my rent -- ?  Do you remember?
Person1: Yes.
Person2: This is really weird and really embarrassing for me but....uh....  I was wondering if you could help me out with a little bit of money.
Person1: Me?
Person2: Yeah.
Person1: I can't really. Yeah, no. I mean. I can't afford it.
Person2: You don't even know how much it is.
Person1: I know but I....how much is it?
Person2: Like seven-fifty. Seven hundred fifty?
Person1: Yes, no, yes. I can't. I can't afford that. I'm sorry. Sorry.
Person2: Really? Please?
Person1: You have trouble, financial trouble?
Person2: Yeah. It's so hard these days and I really need it.
Person1: Yes I can't....I don't make enough money to be able to do that.
Person2: I thought you had your own business. You said you were gonna diversify and all that stuff....

Person1: I'll think you'll find everything in working order. Molly's just having a little fun.
Person2: I don't call this fun, pal. You're playing God with my body.
Person1: You're a substance abuser, Case. Your pancreas was shot. Wouldn't have lasted out the year. We did you a favour and liberated you from a dangerous dependency in the bargain.
Person2: Thanks, but I was enjoying that dependency.
Person1: Good, because you've got a new one.

Person1: I gave you that tongue and I can rip it out!
Person2: No, I really like my tongue... we're very attached.  Oyyyy... Okay, now... promise you won't get angry.
Person1: Why would I ever be angry with you, little friend?

Person1: Your gun, your prints. The Tessiers might overlook a little child molesting, but murder's bad P.R., Mr. Riviera. I suggest to reconsider my offer.
Person2: You son-of-a-bitch, you're crazy.
Person1: What?

Person1: Say -- "What do you want from me, Catherine?"
Person2: What do you want from me, Catherine?

Person1: Addison wants me to play it.
Person2: Over my dead body...
Person1: That won't be necessary. Addison knows how Margo happen to miss that performance - how I happened to know she'd miss it in time to call him and notify every paper in town...  ... it's quite a story. Addison could make quite a thing of it - imagine how snide and vicious he could get and still write nothing but the truth. I had a time persuading him...  ... you'd better sit down. You look a bit wobbly.  If I play "Cora," Addison will never tell what happened - in or out of print. A simple exchange of favors. And I'm so happy I can do something for you - at long last...  Your friendship with Margo - your deep, close friendship - what would happen to it, do you think, if she knew the chap trick you'd played on her - for my benefit? And you and Lloyd - how long, even in the Theater, before people forgot what happened - and trusted you again?  No... it would be so much easier on everyone concerned, if I were to play "Cora." And so much better theater, too...

Person1: Go fuck yourself...
Person2: Hey now!  No cussing on TV!  Well in closing I'd like to say that there are two reasons to have that money outside in an hour.  One is my perky co-anchor here, and the second is the lives of tens of thousands of people--people just like you and me!

Person1: Go on back to the hotel.
Person2: Please, Eddie, don't beg him.
Person1: Would you go on back to the hotel? Take a cab, go on back to the hotel.
Person2: Doesn't all of this come through to you, Eddie? Doesn't any of this mean anything to you? That man, this place, the people. They wear masks, Eddie. And underneath the masks they're perverted, twisted, crippled.
Person1: Shut up.

Person1: Look, I've got to go.  I'm gonna be late for the play.
Person2: Are you going with Chet?
Person1: Chet?  To a play? Are you kidding?
Person2: Then come with me.
Person1: Knox, you are so infuriating!
Person2: Just give me one chance.  If you don't like me after tonight, I'll stay away forever.
Person1: Uh-huh.
Person2: I promise.  Dead Poets honor.  Come with me tonight, then if you don't want to see me again, I swear I'll bow out.
Person1: God, if Chet found out he'd...
Person2: Chet won't know anything.  We'll sit in back and sneak away as soon as it's over.
Person1: Knox, if you promise that this will be the end of it-
Person2: Dead Poets honor.
Person1: What is that?
Person2: My Word

Person1: I'11 be coming around your stateroom in exactly fifteen minutes, Miss Mason. You'd better have your biology project ready or I'm phoning your parents.
Person2: They're out of town.
Person1: Then I'll make sure you remain on board while your classmates see the sights.

Person1: A name. Grunemann. Nothing. And they showed me pictures like this and they meant nothing. Then they asked me, well had I been getting letters, from someone out there in Cabbageville --
Person2: -- Tuscarora --
Person1: All right, yes, I had been. Those sick, wild letters -- I'm watching you, gonna follow you, gonna punish you, kill you et cetera. Well, they said, all right that's Grunemann. So try to remember when you and he - when -- well I don't know, there was that dumper once, he sounded like that dumper --  Dumpers; they get their kicks beating you up. A man hired me once, then tried to really kill me - that'd be about two years ago.

Person1: I need you to remember me, Kate.  How I am right now, right this very moment.  I need you to put that image in your heart and keep it with you, no matter what happens.
Person2: Are you okay, Jack?
Person1: Please, just promise me youll do that. You have to promise, Kate. Because if you dont, then its like it never happened and I dont think I could live with that.

Person1: I told you. If we... you know do it, they go away until ... we're done.
Person2: I know, but even if they go away, everybody in America knows what we're doing because... they went away.
Person1: So? What do they think -- we're not kids --
Person2: I know, I ...
Person1: Shari, I really like you...
Person2: I really like you too...
Person1: ...if this ...  ... weren't here... ?
Person2: ... yeah, then, but...
Person1: So...?
Person2: Ed... I think we should stop seeing each other.

Person1: Oh, I'm sorry, Trip. What you made was a big, brainless, pile of horse shit. No offense.
Person2: Guys, GUYS! Come on, if this is anyone's fault, it's mine. I was the one who grabbed Trip's jacket by mistake. It's my fault and I apologize.
Person1: Please, Jam, we're trying to vent some hostility here. Sure the whole thing may be your fault, but who's gonna get pissed off at you?

Person1: You don't remember me, Mr. Szpilman?
Person2: No, I don't think so?
Person1: Warsaw Radio. I was a technician. I saw you almost every day.
Person2: Sorry, I don't remember.
Person1: Doesn't matter. You've nothing to worry about. I'll visit often.

Person1: Rose, this is Waski. His real name is Warren but we call him Waski. He doesn't like it much and I suppose someday we'll have to stop calling him that.
Person2: You can stop it right now.
Person1: When he was baby we called him "Wa Wa," a baby name, you know. You will find him a very good boy. His brother can be bad and so can his sister, but Waski is a very good boy. And as you can see he's beautiful.
Person2: Oh, Mother, cut it out.
Person1: There are plenty of girls who would give thousands of dollars to have your auburn hair. Beauty is beauty and that's all there is to it. Beauty is there and we have to recognize it. Now Rose, the little girl sitting across from you with the blue eyes is Doll Baby. She looks like an angel and she is an angel, but she can be a naughty angel sometimes, although her father won't believe it. Her real name is Frances, but we call her Dolly or Doll.

Person1: Why can't he help me now?  He's sitting two feet away from me!
Person2: Come on, Quentin, isn't that pretty selfish, putting yourself ahead of the mission?
Person1: Look, I'm shot - it hurts.
Person2: Lieutenant Quiffer, we're all sorry you were hit, and we'll help you stick it together as soon as we get squared away.
Person1: Look, can't someone help me?

Person1: Yes.  It is, George.  Yes.  It's a big decision.  And it's a big reward.  It's a big reward.  For one night's work.  But it's got to be tonight.
Person2: What?
Person1: What?  What?  The leads.
Person2: You have to steal the leads tonight?
Person1: That's right, the guys are moving them downtown.  After the thirtieth. Murray and Mitch.  After the contest.
Person2: You're, you're saying so you have to go in there tonight and...
Person1: You...
Person2: I'm sorry?
Person1: You.

Person1: Let me worry about that, Major. I've still got my red line to the Air Command.
Person2: That's right, sir.
Person1: We don't want to be vulnerable to saboteurs calling up and pretending to be different people from the President down, do we?
Person2: You're right, sir.
Person1: No calls from inside out.  No calls from outside in are even answered.  No calls.
Person2: I understand, sir.  Nothing comes or goes without your personal say-so.
Person1: No calls at all.  With or without my say-so. My voice can be imitated too, Major.
Person2: Yes, sir.  I just thought of something, sir.  How do I know I'm talking to you now?
Person1: Who do you think you're talking to?
Person2: To you, sir.  But how do I know?
Person1: Are you trying to be insubordinate?
Person2: No, sir.
Person1: I hope not.  Now, as soon as you do what I told you, have Plan-R radioed to the Wing.
Person2: Plan-R????
Person1: Are you hard of hearing, Major?
Person2: No, sir.  Plan-R to be radioed to the Wing.
Person1: As soon as you've done that, shut down the communications center.  Lock it up and assign the personnel to base security details.
Person2: General Ripper, if I shut down the communi- cations center, there'll be no radio or teleprinter contact with Air Command head- quarters or anyone, for that matter.
Person1: Are you questioning my orders, Major?
Person2: No, sir.  I'm just bringing the facts to your attention, sir.
Person1: You're a good officer, Major, and you're perfectly right to bring these facts to my attention.
Person2: Thank you, sir.
Person1: Now, as soon as you've done that, double- up on all base security teams.  Our enemies are plenty smart, and there might even be an attack on the base by saboteurs.
Person2: Yes, sir.
Person1: And lastly, all privately owned radios are to be immediately impounded.  They can be used to issue instructions to saboteurs. Air Police will have lists of all owners.

Person1: I didn't get a thing.  Just the music lessons.  That's all there was to it.
Person2: He married you, didn't he?
Person1: He was in love with me.  But he never told me so until after it all came out in the papers about us - and he lost the election and that Norton woman divorced him.
Person2: What about that apartment?
Person1: He wanted me to be comfortable - Oh, why should I bother?  You don't believe me, but it's true.  It just happens to be true.  He was really interested in my voice.  What are you smiling for?  What do you think he built that opera house for?  I didn't want it.  I didn't want to sing.  It was his idea - everything was his idea - except my leaving him.

Person1: An old friend. I was rummaging through a closet and it was just sitting there. Like it was waiting for me.
Person2: It's... it's nice, Paul. It's got... character.
Person1: When I wrote my first book, I used to carry it around in this while I was looking for a publisher. That was a good book, Marcia. I was a writer then.
Person2: You're still a writer.
Person1: I haven't been a writer since I got into the Misery business--
Person2: Not a bad business. This thing would still be growing, too. The first printing order on Misery's Child was the most ever--over a million.
Person1: Marcia, please.
Person2: No, no. Misery Chastain put braces on your daughter's teeth and is putting her through college, bought you two houses and floor seats to the Knick games and what thanks does she get? You go and kill her.
Person1: Marcia, you know I started "Misery" on a lark. Do I look like a guy who writes romance novels? Do I sound like Danielle Steel? It was a one- time shot and we got lucky. I never meant it to become my life. And if I hadn't gotten rid of her now, I'd have ended up writing her forever.  For the first time in fifteen years, I think I'm really onto something here.
Person2: I'm glad to hear that, Paul, I really am. But you have to know--when your fans find out that you killed off their favorite heroine, they're not going to say, "Ooh, good, Paul Sheldon can finally write what we've always wanted. An esoteric, semi-autobiographical character study.
Person1: Marcia, why are you doing this to me? Don't you know I'm scared enough? Don't you think I remember how nobody gave a shit about my first books? You think I'm dying to go back to shouting in the wilderness?  I'm doing this because I have to.  Now, I'm leaving for Colorado to try to finish this and I want your good thoughts--because if I can make it work ...  I might just have something that I want on my tombstone.

Person1: I said, let's go.
Person2: Joey, relax. You're taking this the wrong way. Why don't you sit down and have a drink?
Person1: Excuse me, I'm talking to my sister in-law.
Person2: Excuse me for living.
Person1: What do you think, I'm blind? My brother's breaking his ass in a ring, and you're here with his wife.
Person2: Hey Joey, I'm here with Patsy and Vera and Sandy. And Vickie just happened to come along. We're just trying to have a good time. What do you want from me? So, why don't you just take it easy before this gets out of hand.

Person1: What are you doing here?
Person2: I wanted to see you.
Person1: Okay, you've seen me. Now go away.
Person2: David, I'm sorry I upset you yesterday, but you must understand what is going on.
Person1: I understand all right. You're one of the undead and I'm a werewolf.
Person2: Yes.
Person1: Get out of here, Jack!
Person2: David, tomorrow night is the full moon. You'll change, you'll become...
Person1: A monster. I know, I know.
Person2: You must take your own life now, David, before it's too late.
Person1: Jack, are you really dead?
Person2: What do you think?
Person1: I think I've lost my mind. I think you're not real. I think I'm asleep and you're a part of another bad dream.
Person2: You must believe me.
Person1: What, Jack? That tomorrow night beneath the full moon I'll sprout hair and fangs and eat people? Bullshit!
Person2: The canines will be real. You'll taste real blood! God damit, David, please believe me! You'll kill and make others like me! I'm not having a nice time, David! Don't allow this to happen again! You must take your own life!
Person1: I will not accept this! Now go away!

Person1: I thought I told everybody to get out of my house. That includes cocksuckers and retards. Get off your asses and go.
Person2: This is not your house, Doyle. This is my house and I'll say who stays and goes. You've got a house, why don't you go get one of your girlfriends and go home to it.
Person1: You know better than to talk like that when I'm hurtin'. Don't make me knock the piss out of you.

Person1: We could do it now, we can go upstairs.  ... Please, it's time for us. Come upstairs.
Person2: I like to watch.
Person1: Watch? You mean just watch me? Doing it alone?
Person2: Yes. I like to watch very much.
Person1: Well, if that's what you want, then I want it too.  We can go this way.
Person2: I want to tell Eve.
Person1: Tell Eve? You mean Mrs. Rand?
Person2: Yes.
Person1: Oh, you can tell her later. She'll never miss you in this crowd.

Person1: Rose.
Person2: Kitty...
Person1: Rose, we have a crisis.  I am sure that you are aware of the horrible allegations against Jim Cunningham.
Person2: Yes, I saw the news. Something about a kiddie-porn dungeon.
Person1: Please! Don't say those words.  Well... as you can see... many of us are devastated by this news. This is obviously some kind of conspiracy meant to destroy an innocent man. And I have taken it upon myself to spearhead the Jim Cunningham defence campaign. But unfortunately my civic duties have created a conflict of interest... which involves you.
Person2: Beg pardon?
Person1: Rose... I have to appear at his arraignment tomorrow morning. And as you know, the girls also leave for Los Angeles tomorrow morning. Now, as their coach... I was the obvious choice to chaperone them on the trip.
Person2: But now you can't go.
Person1: Yes. And believe me, of all the other mothers I would never dream of asking you, given the predicament with your son. But none of the other mothers are able to go.
Person2: Oh, Kitty, I don't know. This is so last-minute... Eddie is in New York...
Person1: Rose... I don't know if you realise how great an opportunity this is for our daughters. This has been a dream of ours for a long time.  Sometimes I doubt your commitment to Sparkle Motion.

Person1: I don't know what kind of game you're playing here but I'm out because you are a whack-job.
Person2: He was working on something called Project Manticore, which was using recombinant DNA to produce a superior human...a warrior...an advanced infantry soldier.
Person1: Not that I don't enjoy a good urban legend now and then but what does any of this have to do with me?
Person2: The bar code on your neck, Max.  I know who you are and I know who you're running from.

Person1: Jesus! Listen to this one: "Do you remember me? Airport shuttle, June 7th. You: striking redhead with yellow dress, pearl necklace, brown shoes. I was the bookish fellow in the green cardigan who helped you find your contact lens. Am I crazy, or did we have a moment?"
Person2: God, that's so pathetic. I bet she didn't even notice him.
Person1: I know. And he's like psychotically obsessing over every little detail.
Person2: We should call him and pretend to be the redhead.
Person1: Oh, we totally have to.

Person1: She's in love.
Person2: You're crazy.  I think you're in love.

Person1: I'm sixteen, Keri.  I should be able to live wherever I want.
Person2: And I should have a son who calls me "Mom".  Looks like we're both shit out of luck.
Person1: Okay, you win.  I'll call you Mom. Now can I move into the dorms?
Person2: No.

Person1: Well don't you?
Person2: I think you should try not to think about this anymore.
Person1: Really?
Person2: Yeah.
Person1: Oh. Okay. I'll try that then.

Person1: What did you do that for?
Person2: If you didn't want to go out with me anymore, why didn't you just say it? Instead, you beat around the bush and see that woman behind my back!
Person1: What're you talking about?
Person2: You've been talking to her on the phone for weeks!
Person1: It was only a few times...
Person2: And then you pull that shit this morning, freaking out because I've gone down on a couple guys!
Person1: A couple...?
Person2: I'm not the one trying to patch things up with my ex, sneaking around behind your back! And if you think that being with thirty-seven people is a lot, then just wait, mister.
Person1: Would you let me explain...
Person2: Explain what? How you were waiting until the time was right, and then you were going to dump me for her?
Person1: Veronica... I... it's not like that anymore... I mean, it was never really like that...

Person1: Would you mind walking ahead of me to the sun porch. I feel funny about coming into a dark house.
Person2: But it's light in there now.
Person1: Please.

Person1: Merde... Wait here. When I return you tell me about last night, huh?
Person2: But... I don't remember anything, I swear...
Person1: I leave these open. Maybe something comes back to you.

Person1: You had me worried to death!
Person2: But Sean said...
Person1: I'm the one you should be listening to! Do you think dropping an anchor in the middle of a storm makes any sense whatsoever?

Person1: Who are you calling, Miss Kubelik?
Person2: My sister -- she'll want to know what happened to me.
Person1: Wait a minute -- let's talk this over first.  Just what are you going to tell her?
Person2: Well, I haven't figured it out, exactly.
Person1: You better figure it out -- exactly. Suppose she asks you why you didn't come home last night?
Person2: I'll tell her I spent the night with a friend.
Person1: Who?
Person2: Someone from the office.
Person1: And where are you now?
Person2: In his apartment.
Person1: His apartment?
Person2: I mean -- her apartment.
Person1: What's your friend's name?
Person2: Baxter.
Person1: What's her first name?
Person2: Miss.

Person1: Hey, 'Top.'  What's the op?
Person2: Rescue mission.  There's some juicy colonists' daughters we gotta rescue from virginity.

Person1: You're better when you take them.
Person2: I know, I know.  But still, somehow I don't feel better.
Person1: All right.  How about I feel better when you take them?

Person1: Whats wrong with you?  Are you crazy?
Person2: A little. Does it matter? After all, whats the point of living if you cant feel alive?  Isnt that right, Elektra? Isnt that your motto?
Person1: What are you talking about?
Person2: Or did you steal it from your old friend Renard?
Person1: ...what?
Person2: We had a run-in, he and I. He knew about us, he knew about my shoulder, he knew exactly where to hurt me...

Person1: How can you have any happiness after that? All through the years you'll remember that a man went to the gallows because you were too selfish to wait two hours! I tell you, Earl Williams' face will come between you on the train tonight -- and at the preacher's tomorrow -- and all the rest of your lives!
Person2: What a performance! Bravo! Don't let him fool you, Bruce -- it's only an act!
Person1: What do you mean, only an act? Haven't you got any feeling?
Person2: Well, it's either an act on your part or a miracle on Sweeney's.
Person1: What do you mean?
Person2: I happen to know Sweeney was married only three months ago. If he's got twins this morning, I claim it was done with mirrors.
Person1: All right, Hildy, I'm licked. But I'll make you and Bruce a business proposition.
Person2: We're not interested.
Person1: Maybe you'll be. You're a smart young man. You let Hildy do this story for me and you can write out a $100,000.00 insurance policy for me. What do you say?

Person1: Can I get that credit card from you?
Person2: I don't like to use that credit card, Dignan.
Person1: Why not?
Person2: Because my mom gets the bill.
Person1: She's not going to notice, Anthony.
Person2: I don't want to use it.
Person1: Well, then cut it in half.
Person2: I keep it for emergencies.
Person1: Anthony, we're on the run. This is an emergency. It's only fair that...

Person1: Heartwarming. Really, I feel weepy.
Person2: You wanted to know what happened after the line-up, I'm telling you.
Person1: Oh come on, Verbal. Who do you think you're talking to? You really expect me to believe he retired? For a woman? Bullshit. He was using her.
Person2: He loved her.
Person1: Sure. And I'm supposed to believe that hitting the Taxi Service wasn't his idea

Person1: Why do you look at me like that?
Person2: I did not know you were so beautiful... and --
Person1: And then --?
Person2: No irony. You're so appealing -- so soft -- so tired. I feel like taking you in my arms and not letting anything more happen to you -- ever.
Person1: And -- and --
Person2: How tired you are!
Person1: Yes -- tired...
Person2: So alone.
Person1: Alone. All alone.  Oh, you strange -- strange creature.
Person2: You mustn't talk Russian to me.
Person1: Strange man...
Person2: Am I quite strange to you?
Person1: Not quite strange now. It is as if I had been expecting you. You know, once when the Grand Duke was alive, I found a man hiding in my room -- a young officer --
Person2: And...?
Person1: He disappeared. Later he was found dead.
Person2: I never knew it was so dangerous to hide in a woman's room when she's alone.
Person1: Go away. Who are you --?
Person2: A man who could love -- that is all, who has forgotten everything else for you.
Person1: You could love me. It is so long since I have heard that word. Nobody has loved me for a long time. It is so icy-cold to be famous. One is so cruelly alone. How is it that you -- Let me look at you. Your hands. Your eyes. Why could you love me?
Person2: I saw you just now -- then I saw you cry -- and now I see you in the mirror -- Grusinskaya...
Person1: Grusinskaya... Oh -- oh if you knew how I slaved and slaved for Grusinskaya -- for the success of Grusinskaya -- for the triumph of Grusinskaya... and what is she now? Just someone who has found that on the day success ceases life ceases -- Are you listening to me -- Do you understand? -- I want you to understand.
Person2: Yes -- I do understand.
Person1: I think you must go now -- the key is on the floor.
Person2: I'm not going -- You know I'm not going -- Let me stay here?
Person1: I want to be alone.
Person2: That is not so -- you don't want to be alone.
Person1: I want to be alone --
Person2: No -- You don't want to be alone at all -- You were in despair before -- If I left you, you'd feel worse than you did before, You must not be alone -- You mustn't cry -- you must forget... Tell me that I can stay with you -- tell me.
Person1: Just for a minute then.

Person1: I know!  I-I-I-I, I realize.
Person2: What do you expect me to say?
Person1: Hannah and I are in the last stages.
Person2: Wh-- She's never said anything, and we're very close.  She'd tell me such a thing.
Person1: Wh--, it-it-it-it, it's so sad. She's crazy about me, but somewhere on the, along the line, I've fallen out of love with her.
Person2: Not because of me, I hope.
Person1: Oh, no, no.  Well, yes!  I love you.
Person2: Oh, I can't be the cause of anything between you and Hannah.  I jus--
Person1: Oh, no, no, no.  It, uh, it-it-it- it was i-inevitable that Hannah and I part, anyway.
Person2: Why?
Person1: Tch, w-well, for a million reasons.
Person2: But not over me?
Person1: Tch, no!  We were, we were both going in different directions.
Person2: Poor Hannah.

Person1: That... huh... they just shoot you with drugs.

Person1: Yeah...

Person1: Fine, thank you.  I'm in trouble. That is... now I am!
Person2: Yeah... I know.
Person1: I don't know what I'm gonna do... you know.  Boy... I'm dying.
Person2: What?  What are you talking about? You are dying?  Did you ever listen to yourself when you say that?
Person1: What are you talking about?
Person2: What do you mean... what am I talking about?  Do you realize that you say that to me every day of your life?  I am dying.  Do you know... do you realize the death that you are spreading around to the people who are around you?
Person1: Now don't give me that deep shit now.  Don't start with that shit.
Person2: No really... I don't think that you realize what it means.  The things that you do, Sonny.  You put a gun to somebody's head...
Person1: I don't know what I'm doing.
Person2: Yeah... obviously you don't... when you put a gun to somebody's head... and you say go to sleep so that it won't hurt when I pull the trigger. Death?  Don't talk about death to me.  I have been living with death for the last six months.  Why do you think I'm in the hospital?  I take a handful of pills to get away from you.  And then here I am out of the hospital talking to you on the phone... again.  I have no friends left.  No job.  I can't live.  I have to live with people. This death business... I'm sorry!
Person1: I'm not on the phone to talk to you about that.  Well, I don't know what to say, Leon.  When you gimme that... when you hit me with that shit.  I mean, what am I supposed to say?
Person2: I'm sorry...
Person1: I told you.  That I got a lot of pressures.  You said to me that you needed money, and I knew that you needed money!  I saw you there lying in the hospital like that... and I said... shit, man, I got to get this guy some money.

Person2: Well...
Person1: I did it on my own.  I did this all on my own.  I ain't laying it on anybody.  Nothing on anybody.  I'll tell you something, though, it's about time that I squared away my accounts... you know.  I am squaring away my accounts with life.  Maye this whole thing is gonna end, somehow.  Maybe it'll just end! Maybe I'll just close my eyes and the whole thing will be over. That would be all right too!  I said... I thought I would square it away with you... you know?  That I would get you down here and that I would say so long to you... or, if you wanted... you know, to take a trip...
Person2: What trip?
Person1: I'm getting out of here, man.  I'm not going to stay here and I'm not giving up.  I mean, huh, they're going to kill me, anyway.  So forget it!  But, if I can get out of this... I am going to get out. And, how I'm going to do it is to get a jet out of here and I'm flying the fuck out... That's all, Leon.  If you want to come with me, then you're entitled... you can come.  You're free to do what you want.
Person2: I'm free to do what I want?  And you think I would want to go with you some place on a plane?  Where? Where ya going?
Person1: I gotta jet coming here and we're gonna try to get outta this thing.  And we're gonna go, man!
Person2: You're crazy.
Person1: That's it.
Person2: You're really crazy.
Person1: I know!
Person2: Where you gonna go?
Person1: Who knows?  I think we're gonna go ... we worked it out to Algeria.  So, I don't know.  So I'll go to Algeria.

Person1: Huh ... I don't know.  They got Howard Johnson's there.  I don't know why I'm going there for.

Person1: I know that.  I'm warped... I'm warped!

Person2: I don't know... you could have picked a better place.
